name: Joe Newman
abstract: Joseph Dwight Newman (September 7, 1922 – July 4, 1992) was an American jazz trumpeter, composer, and educator, best known as a musician who worked with Count Basie during two periods.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: In the trumpet section were Joe Newman... I said Joe Newman, right? ... We all knew how to act. ... Every now and then Joe Newman would get on me for lateness.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Joe Williams was in the band then, you know? And oh, that was, Thad Jones, Joe Newman, Wendell Culley and myself was the trumpet section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: Through all of my connections, I put together one of the greatest jazz orchestras that ever was. ... Joe Newman was.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: In the group was Zoot Sims, myself, Joe Newman, Billy Taylor, Kai Winding was the leader...
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Al Grey
about_quotes.evidence: And now coming along, but we had such great soloists, that the band could make it by this change, with “Whirly Bird” with Lockjaw Davis and with Snooky Young and Joe Newman see, and Frank Wess and Marshall Royal, Sonny Payne.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Grey interviewed by Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: Anyway, these are the members of Count Basie’s band. These are alumni. Because I think in this picture there’s Joe Newman, Frank Wess, me with hair, I’m sorry Joe Newman, Frank Foster, Benny Powell, Frank Wess.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]
